seoTitle: How to Copy a TradingView Indicator Script in 2026
metaDescription: Want to study or customize a TradingView indicator's Pine Script? Here's the correct way to view, copy, and reuse source code depending on its access type.
intro: Not every indicator on TradingView lets you copy its Pine Script the same way — it depends on whether the script is public, protected, or invite-only. This guide walks through opening an indicator's source code, copying it into the Pine Editor, and what your options are when 'View Source Code' isn't available. It's aimed at Pine Script learners and strategy developers who want to study or adapt existing indicators.

steps.heading: Check if the indicator is public, protected, or invite-only
steps.body: Before trying to copy anything, confirm the indicator's access type, since only public scripts typically allow full source code viewing.

steps.heading: Open the source code
steps.body: For public indicators, use the option to view source code directly from the indicator's chart menu.

steps.heading: Copy the script into the Pine Editor
steps.body: Select and copy the Pine Script text, then paste it into your own Pine Editor window to save or begin editing it.

steps.heading: Save and customize your copy
steps.body: Save the pasted script under your own name in the Pine Editor, then modify it as needed for your own testing or learning purposes.

steps.heading: Handle protected or invite-only scripts appropriately
steps.body: If 'View Source Code' isn't available, recognize that the script is protected or invite-only and its code isn't meant to be copied without permission.

faq.question: Can I copy any indicator's script on TradingView?
faq.answer: No — only public scripts generally allow you to view and copy the full source code; protected and invite-only scripts restrict this on purpose.

faq.question: What does it mean if 'View Source Code' isn't available?
faq.answer: It usually means the indicator is protected or invite-only, and the creator has intentionally restricted access to the underlying Pine Script.

faq.question: Is it legal to copy and reuse someone else's Pine Script?
faq.answer: This depends on the script's license and access type — public scripts are generally open for learning and personal use, but reusing or republishing someone's protected work raises different considerations.

faq.question: Where do I paste a copied script to use it?
faq.answer: You paste the copied Pine Script into TradingView's Pine Editor, where you can save it, edit it, and apply it to your own charts.
